channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Namibia will have its first female leader after VP wins presidential election for the ruling party
item.description: WINDHOEK, Namibia (AP) — Namibia elected its first female leader as Vice President Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah was declared the winner Tuesday of a presidential election last week that was tarnished by technical glitches that caused a three-day extension to allow votes to be cast, and rejected as illegal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes on Gaza hospital wound 3, Netanyahu vows 'iron fist' in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli drone strikes hit Kamal Adwan Hospital on Tuesday, wounding three medical staff at one of the few hospitals still partially operating in the northernmost part of Gaza, the facility’s director said. Dr. Hossam Abu Safiya said the drones were dropping bombs, spraying shrapnel at the hospital.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's leader prompts dismay by briefly declaring martial law. Here's what to know
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Troops surround South Korea's parliament overnight when the president declares martial law. He accuses pro-North Korean forces of plotting to overthrow one of the world’s most vibrant democracies.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu reacts after Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't free hostages
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says President-elect Trump has the emphasis in "the right place" with his latest demand for Hamas to release hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO chief wants less talk of peace process in Ukraine, more arms
item.description: NATO’s Secretary-General Mark Rutte argued that Ukraine needs to be put in a “position of strength” instead of being pushed toward peace talks, while speaking at a Tuesday conference ahead of the organization’s Foreign Ministers meeting this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: South Korea's leader prompts dismay by briefly declaring martial law. Here's what to know
item.description: South Korea's President Yoon Suk Yeol prompted a night of fear and confusion by issuing a sudden declaration of martial law before lifting it in the early hours.
item.dc:creator: Foster Klug, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Demonstrators in Georgia again converge on parliament after the country's EU bid was suspended
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators in the Georgian capital have converged on parliament again in the capital of the South Caucasus nation.
item.dc:creator: Sophiko Megrelidze, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China responds to US chip sanctions, banning exports of key minerals
item.description: China has banned exports of some rare minerals to the United States as tensions over trade heat up after the U.S. issued new restrictions on exports of certain semiconductor chips and equipment to China earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: South Korean parliament votes to defy president by lifting his declaration of martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law late Tuesday, vowing to eliminate “anti-state” forces as he struggles against an opposition that controls the country’s parliament and that he accuses of sympathizing with communist North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US blasts countries that ‘stood in the way of progress to protect their profits’ over collapsed plastic talks
item.description: The White House criticized a group of oil-rich countries as well as corporations who make money from plastic production in a new statement after global talks for a treaty to reduce plastic waste collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: South Korean president declares martial law in move against opposition party
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law on Tuesday and accused the opposition party of taking the parliamentary process hostage with "anti-state" activities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden says the US is 'all in' on Africa during his Angola visit meant to counter China
item.description: President Joe Biden on Tuesday toured a slavery museum in Angola and inspected shackles and a whip, and he spoke of “our nation’s original sin.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Police in Georgia move again to disperse protests over the suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Police in Georgia's capital again moved to disperse thousands of demonstrators on Monday after over 200 people were detained during four previous nights of protests against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A landmark climate change case opens at the top UN court as island nations fear rising seas
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The top United Nations court took up the largest case in its history on Monday, hearing the plight of several small island nations helpless in combating the devastating impact of climate change that they feel endangers their very survival.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protests lay bare a clash of values and interests as Georgia navigates a Russia-West standoff
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Georgia is convulsed by political upheaval again after its pro-Moscow government, fresh off parliamentary elections denounced as rigged by its critics, decided to suspend negotiations for the small former Soviet republic to join the European Union.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to attend reopening of Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris
item.description: President-elect Trump said Monday that he will attend the reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral next weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: French government faces no-confidence vote over Barnier's austerity budget
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French Prime Minister Michel Barnier is bracing for a no-confidence vote this week, a political reckoning almost certain to topple his fragile government and send shockwaves across the eurozone.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't release hostages
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday threatened to unleash “hell” in the Middle East if hostages held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration Jan.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Thousands cheer Biden's arrival in Angola for his long-promised sub-Saharan Africa visit
item.description: LUANDA, Angola (AP) — President Joe Biden arrived for his long-awaited first presidential visit to sub-Saharan Africa on Monday to the cheers of thousands in Angola, where he will highlight an ambitious U.S.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IDF soldiers accuse UN peacekeepers of enabling Hezbollah terrorists amid increasing cease-fire violations
item.description: In southern Lebanon, Israeli forces confront entrenched Hezbollah positions and question UNIFIL’s ability to enforce its mandate even before the cease-fire agreement was drawn up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Putin leader backs harsh crackdown as over 40 people hospitalized amid protests in Georgia
item.description: Dozens are hospitalized after massive protests broke out across Georgia following the pro-Putin government's move halting decadeslong talks on joining the EU amid increasing ties with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vanuatu calls on ICJ to recognize climate change's harm
item.description: Representatives for the island nation of Vanuatu called on the International Court of Justice this week to recognize the harm caused by climate change, the first remarks the United Nations court heard as it considers international obligations to address climate impacts.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, The Hill

item.title: Stars, heads of state, solemn rituals and high-security celebrations for Notre Dame's reopening
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The reopening of Notre Dame this coming weekend is going to be a high-security affair, with a repeat of some measures used during the Paris Olympics and the sealing-off to tourists of the cathedral's island location in the heart of the French capital.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah fires into Israel-held area, its first strike since ceasefire began
item.description: Hezbollah fired into a disputed border zone held by Israel on Monday, the militant group’s first attack since its ceasefire with Israel took hold last week, after Lebanon accused Israel of violating the truce more than 50 times in recent days. Israel says it has acted to thwart potential attacks.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elton John says he has lost his eyesight, struggles to see new musical
item.description: Elton John says he struggled to watch his new musical because he has lost his eyesight after contracting an infection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden travels to Africa where policies were ‘over-promised and under-delivered,' amid massive China expansion
item.description: President Biden visits Angola this week and is spending billions to get better supplies of electric vehicle battery metals, but the admin may have already been beaten by China's investments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgian PM praises country's protest crackdown despite US condemnation
item.description: Georgia, which has been criticized for using "excessive force" to crack down on anti-government protests, has become increasingly authoritarian, according to Western governments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Imprisoned Kremlin critic convicted again, receives 3-year sentence for opposing war in Ukraine
item.description: Imprisoned Kremlin critic Alexei Gorinov was convicted again on Friday and sentenced to three years in prison for opposing Russia's ongoing war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British lawmakers give initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British lawmakers gave initial approval on Friday to a bill to help terminally ill adults end their lives in England and Wales, following an impassioned debate that saw people sharing personal stories of loss and suffering.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Canada is already examining tariffs on certain US items following Trump's tariff threat
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada is already examining possible retaliatory tariffs on certain items from the United States should President-elect Donald Trump follow through on his threat to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a senior official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation
